tracks.meaning: a structure spanning a physical gap, such as a river or ravine, that allows passage over it
the bony structure connecting the upper and lower teeth in the mouth, or a dental prosthetic device
a curved piece of wood or material on a stringed instrument that holds the strings at a fixed height above the fingerboard
the upper part of a ship's side between the gunwale and the waterline
to connect or join two things together, or to serve as a link between disparate elements
a connection or line of communication established between separated parties or systems
a network structure or protocol that connects different computers or networks together
a therapeutic technique or concept used to help span the gap between a patient's current state and desired change
a card game or the act of building sequences of cards in solitaire
to overcome a significant difference or dispute between people or groups
